channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: As Russia threats loom, Finland's people are learning to shoot back
item.description: KERAVA, Finland (AP) — Unsettled by Russia's expansionism and emboldened by its recent accession to NATO, Finland is rallying to strengthen its national self-defense beyond its traditional military capabilities. The popularity of weapons training in the Nordic country has soared in recent months.
item.dc:creator: JAMES BROOKS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The egg industry kills 350 million male chicks a year. New tech offers an alternative
item.description: Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Texas train derails after hitting tractor-trailer, barreling into city building: video
item.description: One person is dead and four others are injured after a train hit a tractor-trailer on the railroad tracks in Pecos, Texas, causing it to derail and strike the city's Chamber of Commerce building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee dog to be reunited with family after agencies team up to rescue him from ravine: video
item.description: A Tennessee dog by the name of Apollo will be back home for the holidays after he was found by local and state agencies injured and stuck in a ravine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The US egg industry kills 350 million chicks a year. New technology offers an alternative
item.description: WILTON, Iowa (AP) — Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A key Trump ally on immigration explains how mass deportations could work
item.description: A Kansas official who’s an informal adviser to President-elect Donald Trump’s transition team on immigration issues doesn’t expect mass deportations to prompt arrests of migrants at sensitive locations such as schools and churches.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Murdered Kansas moms: Suspect admits to stabbing, not killing
item.description: Veronica Butler, 27, and Jilian Kelley, 39, were murdered earlier this year after they traveled from Hugoton, Kansas, to pick up Butler’s children in Oklahoma.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security bill clears first hurdle in Senate
item.description: The Senate voted 73-27 to advance the measure, which could mean higher Social Security benefits for some Americans if passed before Congress leaves this week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Family files lawsuit against school alleging bullying led to son's suicide
item.description: “At each of these schools, Sammy was a well-behaved student with no emotional or disciplinary issues whatsoever,” the lawsuit said.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New charges target ex-Miami congressman for lobbying on behalf of sanctioned Venezuelan tycoon
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A federal grand jury in Washington has returned an indictment against former Congressman David Rivera for failing to register as a foreign agent of a sanctioned Venezuelan media tycoon on whose behalf the Miami Republican allegedly lobbied the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some Walmarts testing body cameras for employees
item.description: "We are always looking at new and innovative technology used across the retail industry,” a Walmart spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Alleged would-be Trump assassin faces new charge in accident that nearly killed child: Florida authorities
item.description: The state of Florida has charged Ryan Wesley Routh with attempted felony murder in connection with a traffic accident that occurred when he fled police after an alleged assassination attempt on former President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Louisiana patient has first severe bird flu case in US: health officials
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service warns of 'smishing,' other holiday scams: What to know
item.description: “While the holiday season offers merriment, joy, and togetherness, fraudsters and their scams can dim the season,” the Postal Service’s federal law enforcement arm warned.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Business partner of missing Suzanne Simpson's murder suspect husband arrested, indicted after texts emerge
item.description: James "Val" Cotter was indicted by a grand jury this week in the case of missing Texas mom and realtor Suzanne Simpson, who disappeared near San Antonio.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, already stuck in space, are hit with new delay
item.description: Astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, the crew of the first manned Boeing Starliner launch in early June, were initially meant to spend only about eight days in space.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Trump names Herschel Walker as ambassador to Bahamas
item.description: Trump had quipped before Election Day that he would put Walker in charge of a new missile defense shield if elected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Grubhub to pay $25M to settle claims of tricking customers and drivers
item.description: Federal Trade Commission officials said Grubhub engaged in illegal practices by misleading customers about the cost of delivery and the benefits of a Grubhub subscription.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: US to pay nearly $116M to settle lawsuits over rampant sexual abuse at California women's prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. government will pay nearly $116 million to resolve lawsuits brought by more than 100 women who say they were abused or mistreated at a now-shuttered federal prison in California that was known as the “rape club” because of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MICHAEL BALSAMO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing the CEO of UnitedHealthcare, was charged in New York with murder as an act of terrorism, prosecutors announced Tuesday. Mangione, 26, is being held without bail in Pennsylvania, where he was arrested at a McDonald’s on Dec.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why shooting down mystery drones carries big risks
item.description: The buzz around the mysterious flurry of drones above New Jersey and other nearby states has prompted some officials, including President-elect Trump, to call for the U.S. military to shoot down the mystery objects. But such measures come with major risks, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, WGN 9

item.title: Shooting in Madison is the latest carnage at a US school or college over the past 25 years
item.description: Hundreds of people in the U.S. have died in shootings over the past 25 years in places like offices, stores and theaters, but the carnage perhaps hits hardest when it happens in schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect charged with killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO as an act of terrorism
item.description: The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO has been charged with murder as an act of terrorism, prosecutors said Tuesday as they worked to bring him to a New York court from a Pennsylvania jail. Luigi Mangione already was charged with murder in the Dec.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump sued the Des Moines Register and its pollster for “brazen election interference” in publishing a survey the weekend before the election that showed Democrat Kamala Harris with a surprising lead of three percentage points in the state. The Register's parent Gannett Co.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of slain Cash App founder Bob Lee reacts to Nima Momeni verdict
item.description: On Tuesday morning, a San Francisco jury announced it had found Nima Momeni guilty of second degree murder in the death of Cash App founder Bob Lee.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, WGN 9

item.title: FTC bans junk ticket, hotel fees
item.description: The final rule will become effective 120 days after its publication in the Federal Register, coinciding with the first few months of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: NBA unveils new All-Star format featuring four-team tournament
item.description: The NBA 's All-Star Game is going to be an All-Star tournament this season, with the league announcing on Tuesday that it has finalized plans to use a different format for the upcoming midseason showcase in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar Mayer accepting applications for the newest class of Wienermobile drivers
item.description: Oscar Mayer is now accepting applications for its coveted “Hotdogger” position, offering recent college graduates the chance to drive the brand’s iconic 27-foot Wienermobile across the country.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Montana judge blocks rule that prevented transgender people from changing their sex on documents
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A state judge in Montana has temporarily blocked policies that prevented transgender people from changing the sex designation on their birth certificates and driver’s licenses.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Shock poll: 41 percent of young voters find killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO acceptable
item.description: Anger over health insurance companies has been in the spotlight after Thompson was fatally shot Dec. 4 in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about the 2 killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A student and teacher were killed and six others — five students and a teacher — were injured in Monday's shooting.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, ABC 4

item.title: Drone detectors in New Jersey have found 'little or no evidence' of wrongdoing, governor says
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Drone-detecting devices deployed in New Jersey in the past week have found “little to no evidence” of anything nefarious or threatening, Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As schools cut back on bus service, parents turn to rideshare apps
item.description: Reliance on school buses has been waning for years as districts struggle to find drivers and more students attend schools far outside their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
